summary: Large Marth fighter decompilation PR that replaced the 3672-line asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmars.s with C sources under src/melee/ft/chara/ftMars. The work split Marth into core, SpecialN, SpecialS, SpecialHi, and SpecialLw compilation units, added a Marth attributes header, wired the new objects into obj_files.mk/e_files.mk, and expanded fighter/common function declarations needed to compile the matched code. The PR body says the author was going top-down through ftmars.s and that more remained, but this slice shows the asm file was fully removed.
what_changed: Deleted asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmars.s, removing 3672 lines of handwritten/disassembled assembly from the build.

Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftMars/ftMars.c with core Marth lifecycle/item/attribute/effect helpers including ftMars_OnDeath, ftMars_OnLoad, held-item callbacks, attribute copy helpers, knockback callbacks, and accessory/effect callbacks.
Added separate C files for Marth special moves: ftMarsSpecialN.c, ftMarsSpecialS.c, ftMarsSpecialHi.c, and ftMarsSpecialLw.c.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftMars/ftMars.h with MarsAttributes, prototypes for the decompiled Marth functions, and dependencies on global, Dolphin types, HSD_GObj, and Fighter.
Updated obj_files.mk and e_files.mk to replace the ftmars.s object with the new ftMars C object files.

Expanded include/functions.h with prototypes needed by the new Marth code, including movement/action helpers, hitbox helpers, ledge/ground/air transition helpers, effect pause/resume, ef_Spawn, and corrected argument/return types such as func_8007500C returning s32 and func_802C8B28 taking u32.

Refined src/melee/ft/fighter.h by changing ftData.ext_attr from s32* to void*, adding Hitbox x914[4], naming x624_lstick_y, exposing x21EC_callback, adding x21C in ftCommonData, and exposing additional input/state fields used by Marth.
Made small downstream field-name/type fallout fixes in ftsandbag, ftzakoboy, ftzakogirl, ftclink, ftkoopa, and ftcliffcommon, for example replacing x624 with x624_lstick_y.
smart_moves: Split one monolithic character asm file into per-move C compilation units, making future matching, review, and symbol lookup easier than keeping all of ftmars in a single source file.
Introduced a Marth-specific header with a MarsAttributes struct instead of repeatedly treating ext_attr as untyped raw data in every special-move file.
Kept unknown function names and label-derived names where semantics were not established, avoiding premature or invented names while still enabling C matching.

Matched callbacks and transient fighter state through existing Fighter fields such as x2200_ftcmd_var*, x2210_ThrowFlags, x2219_flag.bits.b0, x21D4_callback_EnterHitlag, x21D8_callback_ExitHitlag, x21EC_callback, and x2340_stateVar1.
Used explicit function prototypes in include/functions.h to force correct calling conventions and argument passing for the newly decompiled fighter code.
Added a minimal Hitbox struct/array in Fighter so Marth counter logic could pass typed hitbox pointers to func_8007ABD0 instead of relying only on raw offsets.
decomp_lessons: For fighter characters, decompiling by move family is a useful unit boundary: core lifecycle code in ftMars.c and specials in ftMarsSpecialN/S/Hi/Lw kept the replacement of a large asm file organized.

Character special attributes should be modeled as a character-specific struct even when many fields remain named by offsets; this enables typed accesses like attr->x34, attr->x5C, and attr->x64 while preserving matching.
Build-system updates are part of the match: when replacing an asm object, update obj_files.mk and e_files.mk so the C files compile in the correct text order.
Header/struct work often accompanies matching. This PR needed Fighter layout refinements, function prototypes, and common-field names before Marth code could be expressed cleanly in C.
Do not over-name uncertain code. The PR keeps many func_8013xxxx and lbl_8013xxxx names, which is preferable when the diff does not provide strong semantic evidence.
Small field renames in shared structs can require synchronized cleanup in unrelated fighter files, as seen with x624_lstick_y and other Fighter layout changes.
naming_conventions: Character directory uses ftMars with capital M: src/melee/ft/chara/ftMars/.
Character lifecycle names use ftMars_OnDeath and ftMars_OnLoad where the role is known.
Unidentified matched functions retain address-based names such as func_801362B0 and lbl_801365A8.
Move files follow ftMarsSpecialN.c, ftMarsSpecialS.c, ftMarsSpecialHi.c, and ftMarsSpecialLw.c.
Character-specific attributes use the typedef name MarsAttributes, with unknown fields named by offsets such as x0, x34, x5C, and x64.
Fighter fields remain offset-prefixed but gain semantic suffixes when known, such as x624_lstick_y, x2200_ftcmd_var0, x2340_stateVar1, and x21D4_callback_EnterHitlag.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced a whole asm file with matched C rather than interleaving C and asm for ftmars.s.
Preserved order and addresses through address-derived function names and object-list ordering in obj_files.mk/e_files.mk.
Used typed copies of character special attributes: ftMars_OnLoad copies ext_attr into x2D8_specialAttributes2 and assigns x2D4_specialAttributes to that copy.
Used minimal padding/filler in MarsAttributes and Fighter structs to expose only fields required by the match while maintaining offsets.
Used callback assignments to match effect/hitlag behavior, including efLib_PauseAll and efLib_ResumeAll.

Some matching-sensitive expressions were retained in the C diff, including comments like 'required for some reason' and 'required for regalloc', indicating local code-shape constraints rather than clean semantic rewrites.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text in the slice is the author note: "I'm going top-down through ftmars.s. Still a long ways to go."
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename Marth func_8013xxxx/lbl_8013xxxx symbols to semantic names.
Search later history for refinements to MarsAttributes field names and size.
Check whether func_8007ABD0/Hitbox typing introduced here was later generalized or corrected.
Find subsequent PRs touching ftMarsSpecialN/S/Hi/Lw for cleanup after this top-down match.
Search for later changes to Fighter.x21EC_callback and x624_lstick_y naming fallout.
